events.title: Massive Israeli Strikes in Lebanon Amid Fragile Ceasefire
events.desc: Following a US-Iran ceasefire announcement, Israel launched extensive strikes across Lebanon, targeting Beirut's southern suburbs, southern Lebanon, and the Bekaa Valley. Health officials reported over 300 casualties, with at least 182 killed and 890 wounded in Beirut alone. Lebanese officials described the attacks as a 'grave violation' of the ceasefire.

events.title: Israel Plans Direct Talks with Lebanon Post-Strikes
events.desc: Israeli Prime Minister Netanyahu announced plans for direct talks with Lebanon, a development that follows a significant wave of Israeli airstrikes. Despite the strikes and the announcement, Israel's war goals remain unfulfilled, leading to a muted response to the ceasefire.

events.title: Lebanese Ceasefire Misunderstanding Followed by Deadly Israeli Blitz
events.desc: Lebanon believed a ceasefire was in effect after the US-Iran deal, only to be met with intense Israeli airstrikes. Israel clarified that Lebanon was not included in the US-brokered ceasefire, leading to significant casualties and further destabilization.

events.title: Journalist Killed in Israeli Strike in Gaza; Al Jazeera Condemns
events.desc: Mohammed Wishah, a journalist working for Al Jazeera, was killed in an Israeli strike in Gaza. The Israeli military identified Wishah as a 'Hamas terrorist,' a claim Al Jazeera has denied. This incident prompted the WHO to suspend medical evacuations from Gaza after another contractor was killed by Israeli troops.

events.title: US Journalist Kidnapped in Iraq Released
events.desc: US journalist Shelly Kittleson, who was reportedly kidnapped in Iraq, has been released. US officials are coordinating her safe departure from the country.

events.title: Shooting at Israeli Consulate in Istanbul; No Diplomats Present
events.desc: A gunman was killed and two others injured in a shooting incident at the Israeli consulate in Istanbul. The consulate has been unoccupied for the past two and a half years, and no Israeli diplomats are currently stationed in Turkey.

events.title: US-Iran Ceasefire Deal Creates Divisions Among Iran's Hardliners
events.desc: The two-week ceasefire agreement between the US and Iran, which includes the prospect of direct talks, has unsettled hardliners within Iran. This development has angered conservative factions who view direct engagement with the US with suspicion.

events.title: Negotiators Grapple with Gaps in US-Iran Peace Proposals
events.desc: Mediators face a significant challenge in reconciling the US's 15-point peace plan with Iran's 10-point counter-proposals. The divergence in these plans highlights the complexities and differing objectives in reaching a lasting resolution to the conflict.

events.title: US-Iran Ceasefire Deal Offers Trump an Exit, But with Consequences
events.desc: The recent two-week ceasefire between the US and Iran provides a potential exit strategy for the Trump administration from the ongoing conflict. However, the terms of the deal and its implications may fundamentally alter global perceptions of US foreign policy.

events.title: Details Emerge on US-Iran Two-Week Ceasefire
events.desc: A provisional two-week truce has been established between the US and Iran, occurring over a month after coordinated attacks by the US and Israel against Iran. The specifics of this ceasefire are being closely examined as it offers a pause in hostilities.

events.title: Cautious Shipping Amid Fragile Ceasefire in Strait of Hormuz
events.desc: Vessel traffic through the Strait of Hormuz remains subdued, with only a limited number of ships crossing since the US-Iran ceasefire agreement. This indicates ongoing caution among maritime operators despite the pause in conflict.

events.title: Ceasefire Provides Respite, But Long-Term Peace Uncertain
events.desc: The recent ceasefire offers a temporary reprieve for civilians caught in the conflict. However, the article suggests that the underlying issues remain unresolved, and the potential for renewed hostilities persists, reshaping the geopolitical landscape of the Middle East.

events.title: Economic Impact: Oil Prices Plunge, Shares Rise on Ceasefire News
events.desc: The announcement of a US-Iran ceasefire led to a significant drop in crude oil prices, falling by as much as 15%. Stock markets also experienced a surge. Despite the decline, oil prices remain considerably higher than pre-war levels.

events.title: French Couple Released After Over Three Years in Iranian Jail
events.desc: A French couple, Cecile Kohler and Jacques Paris, have been permitted to leave Iran after being held for more than three years. Their release was confirmed by the French president, marking an end to their lengthy detention.

events.title: Enduring Psychological Scars on Iranian Children Post-Conflict
events.desc: The article highlights the lasting psychological trauma experienced by children in Iran due to the ongoing conflict. It presents testimonies from parents and aid workers detailing the distress and long-term impact on young lives, even if active fighting ceases.
